DMEM/F12+++ = DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and
1% pen/step

Fibroblast media = DMEM/F12+++ +10 % FBS

# Sample S460_iOme 

**17.06.2024 - Day 0**

**Fibroblast isolation**

Small tissue pieces were placed in a 6-well plate. Approx. 6 pieces per
well and with 800 ul media per well. No digestion was used.

**18.06.2024 - Day 1**

300 ul medium was added on day 1.

**24.06.2024 - Day 7**

The tissue pieces were removed from the wells as enough fibroblasts had
isolated and attached to the plate. Wells were washed once with PDB and
2 ml fresh media was added.

Media was changed every Mon, Wed, and Fri. Before the media change,
wells were washed once with PBS.

**30.06.2024 - Day 13**

Wells 1 and 2 were trypsinized (0.25% trypsin EDTA, 3 min at +37C) and
pooled together for plating. All of these cells were plated in a cell
culture plate with 5 ml of fresh media.

→ Plate 1: S460 iOme P1 w/o dig. DMEM/F12+++ +10 % FBS 30.06.24 MH

**01.07.2024 - Day 14**

Wells 3, 4, and 5 were trypsinized and pooled together for plating.
Cells were resuspended into 1 ml media and 750 µl of the cell suspension
was plated into a cell culture plate with 5 ml of fresh media.

→ Plate 2: S460 iOme P1 w/o dig. DMEM/F12+++ +10 % FBS 01.07.2024 AE

**02.07.2024 - Day 15**

**Coverslip preparation**

**A**utoclaved coverslips were placed in the wells of a 24-well plate. 6

iOme P1 w/o dig. DMEM/F12+++ +10 % FBS 01.07.2024 AE

**02.07.2024 - Day 15**

**Coverslip preparation**

**A**utoclaved coverslips were placed in the wells of a 24-well plate. 6
wells were coated with Cultrex gel solution, 4 wells were coated with
Omentum gel solution, and 6 wells were left uncoated. All reagents were
kept on ice during preparation.

The final concentration of both gels was determined to be 0.5 mg/ml. See
the calculation for gel solutions below.

Cultrex gel solution was prepared by mixing media and Cultrex gel stock
and left on ice for a few minutes before adding it to the wells. 400 µl
of Cultrex gel solution was added per well. Cultrex gel solution needs
to incubate overnight at +37°C, and the coating solution can be removed
just before cell seeding.

Omentum gel solution was prepared by first adding omentum stock,
aprotinin, and thrombin to the media. After fibrinogen was thawed and
warmed in a +37°C water bath and everything was ready for plating the
omentum gel solution, fibrinogen was added to the solution.

We tried adding 400 µl of Omentum gel solution per well, but it was too
much as it covered the whole well, not just the coverslip. We did new
calculations (seen below) and added 100 µl of Omentum gel solution per
well. That was enough to cover the coverslip with a thin layer of the
gel. However, even less could be enough.

alculations (seen below) and added 100 µl of Omentum gel solution per
well. That was enough to cover the coverslip with a thin layer of the
gel. However, even less could be enough. After 1 hour of incubation at
+37°C, medium was added to the Omentum gel-coated wells.

400 µl PBS was added to the uncoated coverslips/wells.

**03.07.2024 - Day 16**

**Fibroblast purification/enrichment**

We trypsinized both plates 1 and 2 and pooled them together.

Plate 1: S460 iOme P1 w/o dig. DMEM/F12+++ + 10 % FBS 30.06.24 MH

Plate 2: S460 iOme P1 w/o dig. DMEM/F12+++ + 10 % FBS 01.07.2024 AE

After trypsin was stopped with media and cell suspension was centrifuged
at 1500 rpm 5min at +4C, cells were resuspended into 2 ml of media for
cell calculations. Cell count was around 1.4 E6 cells in 2 ml meaning
0.7 E6 cells/ml.

Anti-fibroblast MicroBeads protocol by Miltenyi Biotec was used as
guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared in advance: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and
CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA) to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in
DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and everything else was sterile
except for EDTA.

ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and
CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA) to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in
DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and everything else was sterile
except for EDTA. The prepared buffer was sterile-filtered before using
it.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice when not needed inside
the hood.

After calculations cells were centrifuged at 300 g for 10 min at +4C.
The supernatant was aspirated and the cell pellet was resuspended in 80
µl of cold MACS buffer (commercial protocol suggests 80 µl per 10 E7
cells). To the suspension, 20 µl of Anti-Fibroblast MicroBeads was added
(protocol: 20 µl per 10 E7 cells) Cell suspension was mixed well and
incubated 15 min at +4C (protocol: 30 min at RT). Cells were washed by
adding 1 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 300 g for 10 min at +4C. The
supernatant was aspirated and cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS
buffer.

LS column and 30 µm filter were placed in the magnetic MACS Separator.
Column and filter were prepared by washing it with 3 ml MACS buffer. The
cell suspension was added to the column and washed with 3 x 3 ml MACS
buffer. Collect flow-through to a separate 15 ml falcon tube, this time
cell suspension and washing were collected into the same falcon tube.
Now this suspension should include all cells except fibroblasts i.e.
epithelial cells.

alcon tube, this time
cell suspension and washing were collected into the same falcon tube.
Now this suspension should include all cells except fibroblasts i.e.
epithelial cells.

The column was removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml
falcon tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and with firmly
pushing the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells flushed
out.

Both cell suspensions were centrifuged 300 x for 10 min and then
resuspended in 1 ml media. Cell counts were calculated:

Fibroblasts: 0.7 E7 cells/ml

Epithelial cells 0.407 E7 cells/ml

For plating both fibroblasts and epithelial cells into Cultrex-coated
coverslips and uncoated coverslips, we tested if 5000 cells per well
would be enough (reference:
[[https://www.rndsystems.com/resources/protocols/protocol-preparation-and-fluorescent-icc-staining-cells-coverslips]{.underline}](https://www.rndsystems.com/resources/protocols/protocol-preparation-and-fluorescent-icc-staining-cells-coverslips))

So 7.2 µl of fibroblast cell suspension was plated into 2 wells with
Cultrex coating and into 2 wells without coating. Similarly, 12.3 µl of
epithelial cell suspension was plated into 2 wells with Cultrex and 2
without coating.

We forget to save any cell suspension for the original cell suspension
containing all cell types. But for the tube that had previously

2 wells with Cultrex and 2
without coating.

We forget to save any cell suspension for the original cell suspension
containing all cell types. But for the tube that had previously
contained the whole cell suspension, we added 1 ml media and plated all
that into again 2 wells with Cultrex coating and 2 wells without coating
to see if any cells were left in the tube.

Cell suspension were plated in the middle of the coverslip and after 5
min of incubation at RT, we added 200 µl or 400 µl media to the wells.
This resulted in the cells floating in the media and not staying in top
on the coverslip.

For next time, it would be better to incubate the cells 30 min RT before
adding the media, so that the cells can attach to the coverslip.

The rest of the fibroblast cell suspension (850 µl) was divided into 2
eppis for flow cytometry staining. Rest of the epithelial cell
suspension (750 µl) was transferred into one eppis also for flow
staining.

**NOTE!** Later noticed that to protocol suggests 80 µl MACs buffer per
10 E6 cells, not 10 E7. Same with the recommended amount of beads.

height="5.015625546806649in"}

**FACS staining**

Samples:

1. Eppis: unstained fibroblast cells

2. Eppis: multicolor fibroblast cells

3. Eppis : multicolor epithelial cells

Added 900 µl FACS buffer to all samples and centrifuged 400 g for 6 min
at +4C.

ned fibroblast cells

2. Eppis: multicolor fibroblast cells

3. Eppis : multicolor epithelial cells

Added 900 µl FACS buffer to all samples and centrifuged 400 g for 6 min
at +4C. Removed the supernatant and added 50 µl of surface antibody mix
to multicolor samples. Incubated for 30 min at +4C in the dark.
Resuspended the unstained sample in FACS buffer.

The surface antibody mix contained all antibodies in the FC panel below
except a-SMA.

0.75 µl BV421 CD45

3 µl APC-Cy7 EpCAM

1.5 µl AF700 CD29

1.5 µl PE-Cy7 CD31

3,75 µl PE PDGFR-b

6µl AF488 FAP

in 150 µl Brilliant Stain buffer

![](media/image116.png){width="6.5in" height="1.0555555555555556in"}

After incubation, added 900 µl FACS buffer and centrifuged 400 g 6min at
+4C.

For intracellular staining, 200 µl Fixation buffer to fix the cells.
Incubated for 40 min at +4C shielded from light. After that, 1 ml 1x
Permeabilisation buffer and incubated for 5 min at +4C shielded from
light.

After incubations, centrifuged 400 g 6min +4C, discarded supernatant and
washed the pellet with 1min 1x Permeabilization buffer. Again centifuged
and then resuspended the pellets in 900 µl FACS buffer for overnight
storage at +4C shielded from light.

**04.07.2024 - Day 17**

**FACS staining**

Centrifuged the cells 400 g 6min +4C and prepared the intracellular
antibody mix:

3,75 µl APC a-SMA in 150 µl FACS buffer

rom light.

**04.07.2024 - Day 17**

**FACS staining**

Centrifuged the cells 400 g 6min +4C and prepared the intracellular
antibody mix:

3,75 µl APC a-SMA in 150 µl FACS buffer

Resuspended the pellet in 50 µl of intracellular antibody mix and
incubated 30 min +4C shielded from light.

After incubation, washed the cells with 1 x Permeabilization buffer,
centrifuged 400 g 6 min +4C, and resuspended the pellet in 350 µl FACS
buffer. Pipeted the cell suspension through Falcon 5 ml polystyrene tube
with a cell-strainer cap (blue). Samples were kept on ice and protected
from light until running at Fortessa.

**Compensation beads:**

Added 1 drop of both positive (BD CompBeads AntiMouse IgG,
#51-90-9001229) and negative (CompBeads Negative Control #51-90-9001291)
compensation beads to Falcon 5ml polystyrene round bottom tubes (white
cap). To each compensation tube labeled for a single
fluorochrome-conjugated antibody, added 1 ul of the corresponding
antibody (see FC panel above for the antibodies, except DCM-BV510 was
replaced with CD16-BV510). Compensation tubes were incubated approx. 1
hour 45min at +4 shielded from light before adding 350 ul FACS buffer.
30 min incubation is enough but FACS buffer was added just before
running the compensations at Fortessa.

## Flow cytometry results

No FMO samples were included in this staining, so I used the unstained
sample as a guide for gating.

The samples, which should contain only epithelial cells and only
fibroblasts due to the purification process, look the same as they both
have EpCAM+ cells. Similarly, in both samples, the largest population
consists of EpCAM-, CD45-, and CD31- cells. They also show similar
patterns in the CD29 to FAP and α-SMA to CD29 plots.

Unstained sample:

![](media/image67.png){width="6.5in" height="1.6527777777777777in"}

Stained samples: above fibroblasts and below epithelial cells

![](media/image9.png){width="6.5in" height="3.861111111111111in"}

**10.07.2024 - Day 22**

Media was changed and pictures of the wells were taken.

400 µl per well.

**11.07.2024 - Day 24**

Pictures were taken from the wells.

Well 1 of the epithelial cells without coating was full so decided to
fix that.

**Fixation**

Fixed well 1 epithelial cells without coating

Protocol for fixation was based on this:
[[https://www.rndsystems.com/resources/protocols/protocol-preparation-and-fluorescent-icc-staining-cells-coverslips]{.underline}](https://www.rndsystems.com/resources/protocols/protocol-preparation-and-fluorescent-icc-staining-cells-coverslips)

ion-and-fluorescent-icc-staining-cells-coverslips]{.underline}](https://www.rndsystems.com/resources/protocols/protocol-preparation-and-fluorescent-icc-staining-cells-coverslips)

Before starting, we thawed the 4% PFA in warm water for a few hours.

Transferred the coverslip to a new 24-well plate using tweezers and a
pipet tip. Washed twice with PBS before adding 400 µl 4% PFA for 20 min
at RT. Rewashed twice with PBS and stored in PBS in the fridge, plate
shield with parafilm. Fixed cells can be stored for up to 3 months.

Discarded the remaining PFA in a PFA waste container inside the hood.

**12.07.2024 - day 25**

Media change

**15.07.2024 - day 28**

Well 2 fibroblasts cultrex coating, and wells 1 and 2 fibroblasts
without coating was confluent enough for fixation. Fixed them as
described above.

Changed the media for remaining cells in the 24-well plate with
fibroblasts and epithelial cells growing on cultrex coating or without
any coating/on glass.

**17.07.2024 - day 30**

Well 1 fibroblat cultrex coating was confluent enough for fixation.
Unfortunately dropped and losed the coverslip while transferring is to
the separate 24-well with fixed cells.

Changed the media for remaining wells: wells 1 and 2 epithelial cells
with cultrex coating, and

well 2 epithelial cells without coating

**19.07.2024 - day 32**

th fixed cells.

Changed the media for remaining wells: wells 1 and 2 epithelial cells
with cultrex coating, and

well 2 epithelial cells without coating

**19.07.2024 - day 32**

Changed the media for remaining wells: wells 1 and 2 epithelial cells
with cultrex coating, and well 2 epithelial cells without coating.

**22.07.2024 - day 35**

Changed the media for remaining wells: wells 1 and 2 epithelial cells
with cultrex coating, and well 2 epithelial cells without coating.

**23.07.2024 - day36**

Felt like the cells were not longer growing so decided to fix the wells:
well 1 epithelial cells with cultrex coating, and well 2 epithelial
cells without coating. Well 2 epithelial cells with cultrex coating
didn't really have cells so discarded that one.

**So in total, we have following coverslips with fixed cells in wet lab
fridge:**

Well 2 fibroblasts with cultrex coating

Well 1 fibroblasts without coating

Well 2 fibroblasts without coating

Well 1 epithelial cells with cultrex coating

Well 1 epithelial cells without coating

Well 2 epithelial cells without coating

In total, 4 coverslips were imaged using "Lola" scanner: FG fibroblasts
in glass (without coating), FC: fibroblasts in coated glass (with
cultrex coating), EG = epithelial cells in glass (without coating), EC:
epithelial cells in coated glass (with cultrex coating). Raw images can

: fibroblasts in coated glass (with
cultrex coating), EG = epithelial cells in glass (without coating), EC:

**26.07.2024 - day 39**

**IF staining of PFA fixed cells in coverslips**

Firstly, prepared Blocking buffer (3 % BSA and 2 % TritonX-100 in 1xPBS)
by adding 0.3 g of BSA and 0.2 ml TritonX-100 into 10 ml 1xPBS. To
enable the pipeting of triton, cutted the smalles part of a pipet tip
off. Vortex the solution thoroughly until BSA and triton was dissolved.

Prepared a IF chamber with wet tissue papers and parafilm, and added
coverslips on top to the parafilm. Added 50 µl of the blocking buffer
per coverslip and incubated 30 min RT shielded from light. Removed
blocking buffer by converting the coverslip and tipeting the buffer from
the edge of the coverslip and from the parafilm.

Prepared antibodymix with 1.75 µl PAX8, 1.167 µl FAP and 0.12 µl aSMA
antibodies in 350 µl blocking buffer. Added 50 µl of the antibodymix to
the coverslip and incubated 45 min RT shielded from light. Removed the
antibody by converting the coverslip and washed them by pipetting 100 µl

. Added 50 µl of the antibodymix to
the coverslip and incubated 45 min RT shielded from light. Removed the
antibody by converting the coverslip and washed them by pipetting 100 µl
PBS onto the coverslips while folding them converted. Prepared HOECHST
antibodymix with 35 µl of 1:100 diluted HOECHST mix into 350 µl blocking
buffer. Added 50 µl of HOECHST mix per coverslip and incubated 5 min.

After incubation, washed the coverslips by pipetting 100 µl PBS onto the
coverslips while folding them converted. Removed excess PBS by turning
the coverslip sideways on a paper tissue. Mounted the coverslips by
adding 20 µl glycerol onto the coverslip and placing the coverslips on
to microscopy slides.

# Sample S464_pOvaR

**10.7.24 - day 0**

Copied from S464_pOvaR patient sample protocol:

**Tissue dissociation**

The tumor piece was quite small. I cut it and dissociated it using
Collagenase/Hyaluronidase (#07912 StemCell technologies, aliquoted
12.03.2020 NC), and noticed later that it had expired already 05/2020).
For 10 ml of enzymatic dissociation medium, I used 8,9 ml of DMEM/F12+++
medium, 1 ml C&H (10x to 1x), and 100 ul DNAse (RNase free). Incubated
in +37C for 2 hours rotating; RBC lysis buffer 2 mL + 10 mL of AdF+++
medium. After centrifuge, cells were resuspended in 2 mL of the medium
and counted.

(RNase free). Incubated
in +37C for 2 hours rotating; RBC lysis buffer 2 mL + 10 mL of AdF+++
medium. After centrifuge, cells were resuspended in 2 mL of the medium
and counted.

## Flow cytometry results

The samples included very few stromal cells which can already be seen
from the scatter plot. After gating EpCAM-, CD45- and CD31- cells
(gating is based on FMO samples), very few cells are left.

We suspect that the Collagenase/Hyaluronidase (#07912 StemCell
technologies, aliquoted 12.03.2020 NC) was not working and therefore no
stromal cells were dissociated from the sample.

brighter than the compensations.

# Sample S467_pOme []{.mark}

## diagnosis: no gynecological malignancy, adenocarcinoma

**15.7.24 - day 0**

Copied from S467_pOme patient sample protocol:

The tumor piece was quite small and fatty, there were no leftovers for
others. I cut it into small pieces and separated around 30 small pieces
for plating (see A). Dissociated the remaining using
Collagenase/Hyaluronidase (#07912 StemCell technologies, aliquoted
25.10.23 MH). For 10 ml of enzymatic dissociation medium, I used 8,9 ml
of DMEM/F12+++ medium, 1 ml C&H (10x to 1x), and 100 ul DNAse (RNase
free). Incubated in +37C for 2 hours rotating; RBC lysis buffer 4 mL +
20 mL of AdF+++ medium. After centrifuge, cells were resuspended in 5 mL
of the medium and counted.

**Plating tissue pieces for fibroblast isolation**

Plated small tissue pieces taken from different parts of the tumor
tissue into 2 6cm cell culture plates. 12-15 pieces per plate. Added
approx. 1 ml of AdF+++ medium + 10% FBS media per plate so that the
tissue pieces do not float but the plate is covered with media.

![](media/image68.png){width="2.5in" height="1.2314457567804025in"}

**Plating cells**

5 plates with 1 E6 cells per plate were plated in 10cm cell culture
plates. For this aliquoted cell suspension was centrifuged and
resuspended in 5 ml of AdF+++ medium + 10% FBS. 1 ml per plate of the
cell suspension was added drop by drop to the plate containing 10 ml
AdF+++ medium + 10% FBS media.

From the cell suspension, the volume required for each plate was 0.0427
ml

Volume per plate = 1 x E6 cells​ / 23.4 x E6 cells/ml = 0.0427 ml

**Freezing cells**

4 cryotubes were frozen with each 5 E6 cells. For this aliquoted cell
suspension was centrifuged, resuspended in 4 ml Stem Cell Banker, and
aliquoted into 4 cryotubes.

From the cell suspension, the total volume required for all 4 cryotubes
was 0.8548 ml (4 x 0.2137 ml)

Volume per tube = 5 x E6 cells / 23.4 x E6 cells/ml​ = 0.2137 ml

ryotubes.

was 0.8548 ml (4 x 0.2137 ml)

Volume per tube = 5 x E6 cells / 23.4 x E6 cells/ml​ = 0.2137 ml

**FACS staining with fibroblast flow cytometry panel: surface staining**

11 aliquots with 0.5 E6 cells per tube were aliquoted for FACS staining.

From the cell suspension, the total volume required for all 11 tubes was
0.2354 ml (11x 0.0214 ml)

Volume per tube = 0.5 xE6 cells / 23.4 x E6 cells/ml​ =0.0214 ml

Surface staining:

Added FACS buffer and centrifuged 400 g +4 C 6 min.

Made an antibody mix for the multicolor samples; 50µl per tube, diluted
in Brilliant Stain buffer. For FMO samples, added the needed amount
Brilliant Stain buffer (see table below) to the cells and then added
antibodies one by one to the tubes (containing the cells). For FMO
samples, every other antibody was added except the corresponding one.

Incubated all samples 30 min at +4C and an additional 10 min RT. Washed
and incubated for 40 min with Fixation buffer (#00-8222 Invitrogen) and

dded except the corresponding one.

Incubated all samples 30 min at +4C and an additional 10 min RT. Washed
and incubated for 40 min with Fixation buffer (#00-8222 Invitrogen) and
added 1x permeabilization (10x perm (Invitrogen) buffer diluted in
mQH2O) for 5 min. Centrifuged and resuspended the pellets in FACS buffer
for overnight storage at +4C.

**In addition to all described above, Panos established long-term
organoid cultures of the cells.**

**16.7.24 - day 1**

**FACS staining with fibroblast flow cytometry panel: intracellular
staining and compensations**

Intracellular staining:

Centrifuged the cells 400 g 6min +4C and prepared the intracellular
antibody mix:

15 µl APC a-SMA in 585 µl 1 x Permeabilization buffer.

Resuspended the pellet in 50 µl of intracellular antibody mix or only
permeabilization buffer and incubated 30 min +4C and an additional 10
min RT shielded from light.

After incubation, washed the cells with 1 x Permeabilization buffer,
centrifuged 400 g 6 min +4C, and resuspended the pellet in 400 µl FACS
buffer. Pipeted the cell suspension through Falcon 5 ml polystyrene tube
with a cell-strainer cap (blue).

Samples were kept on ice and protected from light until running at
Fortessa.

Compensation beads:

Added 1 drop of both positive (BD CompBeads AntiMouse IgG,
#51-90-9001229) and negative (CompBeads Negative Control #51-90-9001291)

ntil running at
Fortessa.

Compensation beads:

Added 1 drop of both positive (BD CompBeads AntiMouse IgG,
#51-90-9001229) and negative (CompBeads Negative Control #51-90-9001291)
compensation beads to Falcon 5ml polystyrene round bottom tubes (white
cap). To each compensation tube labeled for a single
fluorochrome-conjugated antibody, added 1 ul of the corresponding
antibody (see FC panel above for the antibodies, except DCM-BV510 was
replaced with CD16-BV510). Compensation tubes were incubated approx.
45min at +4 shielded from light before adding 350 ul FACS buffer.

## Flow cytometry results

Some cells are outside of the scale but the dissociation worked better
(compared to S464_pOvaR) and we can clearly see more stromal cells in
the scatter plot. From EpCAM-, CD45- and CD31- cells, plots with CD29 to
FAP and a-SMA to CD29 show similar patter as seen in Givel et al paper

n clearly see more stromal cells in
the scatter plot. From EpCAM-, CD45- and CD31- cells, plots with CD29 to
FAP and a-SMA to CD29 show similar patter as seen in Givel et al paper
[[https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-018-03348-z]{.underline}](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-018-03348-z).

Conclusion is that FSC and SSC voltages need to be smaller. Maybe FSC
150 to 100 and SSC 230 to 210.

From Givel et al: Figure 1. d Gating strategy to identify CAF subsets in
HGSOC by FACS. Results from a representative HGSOC patient are shown.
Cells isolated from freshly dissociated human HGSOC were first gated on
DAPI−, EPCAM−, CD45−, CD31− cells, for excluding dead cells (DAPI+),
epithelial cells (EPCAM+), hematopoietic cells (CD45+), and endothelial
cells (CD31+). Selected cells were next examined using six fibroblast
markers. Representative flow cytometry plots show gating strategies
based on FAP, CD29, SMA, and FSP1 that allow the identification of four
sub-populations of fibroblasts in HGSOC: CAF-S1 are CD29Med FAPHigh
SMAHigh FSP1High, CAF-S4 are CD29High FAPLow SMAHigh FSP1Med, CAF-S3 are
CD29Low FAPLow SMALow FSP1Med/High and CAF-S2 are CD29Low FAPLow SMALow
FSP1Low.

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples and washed the larger tumor pieces
 > by placing them into a 50 ml Falcon tube, pouring sterile HBSS on
 > top, and gently inverting the tube several times.

- Approx. ⅔ of the tumor pieces were sliced and divided into two 50 ml
 > Falcon tubes for conditions 1&2 and 3&4.

- The remaining ⅓ of the tumor pieces were minced into smaller pieces
 > using a sterile scalpel and scissors, and then transferred into a
 > 50 ml Falcon tube for conditions 5 and 6.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated 1&2 and 3&4 with 0.125 % trypsin for 20 min in +37°C water
 > bath.

 - 0.125 % trypsin solution: 10 ml DPBS (without MgCl2 and CaCl2) +
 > 10 ml 0.25% trypsin EDTA

 - Gently inverted the tubes a few times after 10 min and at the
 > end of the incubation period.

- After incubation, took the tissue pieces out and placed them into

rypsin EDTA

 > end of the incubation period.

- After incubation, took the tissue pieces out and placed them into
 > two cell culture dishes for mincing. To the two cell suspension,
 > added 20 ml DMEM/F12+++ media each, centrifuged 300 g 5 min RT,
 > and resuspended both tubes in 1 ml. Pooled the cell suspension
 > together and marked this cell suspension as sample 1-4.

- Minced the sliced tissue pieces into tiny pieces, and divided the
 > contents of the two plates into two 50 ml Falcon tubes each. In
 > summary, we had separate 50 ml Falcon tubes for each condition (1
 > to 4)

- Treated samples with C&H for the specified durations (at +37°C
 > rotating). Vortexed approx. 15 times before and after the
 > incubation period.

 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples with a 70 µm cell strainer to obtain
 > single-cell suspensions

 > 1000 rpm at +4°C

**Fibroblast purification**

Tried again fibroblast purification for cells fron conditions 1-5 pooled
together. Anti-fibroblast MicroBeads protocol by Miltenyi Biotec was
used as guidance.

Used the previously prepared MACS buffer, but re- sterile-filtered
before using it.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice when not needed inside
the hood.

After calculations cells were centrifuged at 300 g for 5 min RT. The
supernatant was aspirated and the cell pellet was resuspended in 80 µl
of cold MACS buffer (Commercial protocol suggests 80 µl per 10 E7 cells.
Our cell count was much less than E7, but decided to try this for now
and next time could decrease the volume of the buffer and beads.) To the
suspension, 20 µl of Anti-Fibroblast MicroBeads was added (protocol: 20
µl per 10 E7 cells) Cell suspension was mixed well and incubated 30 min
at RT. Cells were washed by adding 1 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 300
g for 10 min. The supernatant was aspirated and cells were resuspended
in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

LS column and 30 µm filter were placed in the magnetic MACS Separator.

ifuged 300
g for 10 min. The supernatant was aspirated and cells were resuspended
in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

LS column and 30 µm filter were placed in the magnetic MACS Separator.
Column and filter were prepared by washing it with 3 ml MACS buffer. The
cell suspension was added to the column and washed with 3 x 3 ml MACS
buffer. Collected the flow-through to a 15 ml falcon tube which should
include all cells except fibroblasts. The column was removed from the
separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was
added to the column and with firmly pushing the plunger into the column,
magnetically labeled cells flushed out. Both cell suspensions were
centrifuged 300 g for 10 min, and resuspended in 500 µl of DMEM/F12+++
media.

**Copied from Dissociation_protocol_optimization_S473_pOme:**

Approaches:

1\. \*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

- Condition 1: 30 min 0.125 % trypsin and 1 hour C&H

- Condition 3: 30 min 0.125 % trypsin, 1 hour C&H, followed by brief
 > (15 sec) 0.125% trypsin of the cell pellet

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

- Condition 6: Dispase for 30 min, followed by C&H for 1 hour

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples and washed the larger tumor pieces
 > by placing them into a 50 ml Falcon tube, pouring sterile HBSS on
 > top, and gently inverting the tube several times.

- The tumor piece was cut in three adjacent parts

- Two pieces were added into a 50 ml Falcon tube containing 20 mL of
 > 0.125 % trypsin in PBS for conditions 1 and 3.

- The remaining piece was minced into small pieces using a sterile
 > scalpel and scissors, and then transferred into a 50 mL Falcon
 > tube for condition 6.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- For condition 1&3, treated with 0.125 % trypsin for 30 min in +37°C
 > water bath.

dition 6.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- For condition 1&3, treated with 0.125 % trypsin for 30 min in +37°C
 > water bath.

 - 0.125 % trypsin solution: 10 ml DPBS (without MgCl2 and CaCl2) +
 > 10 ml 0.25% trypsin EDTA

 - Gently inverted the tubes a few times every 10 min and at the
 > end of the incubation period.

- After incubation, took the tissue pieces out and placed them into a

## Flow cytometry results 060824

Used for the first time BD CompBeads Plus (7.5 µm) compensation beads.
Now with these beads, the compensation control are looking better since
the compensation controls are brighter than the sample with most of the
antibodies. Still, aSMA still seems to be brighter in the sample than in
the control, and with current voltages sSMA is going over the scale.

In addition, the PDGFR-b seems to not be working in our samples.

# Sample S481_pAdnR 

**26.08.2024 - day 0 and 27.08.2024 - day 1**

**Copied from Dissociation_protocol_optimization_S481_pAdnR:**

Approaches:

1\. \*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples

- Mince the tumor piece into small pieces using sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\.

ion\*:

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated sample with C&H for 1 hour at +37°C rotating. Vortexed
 > before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filter the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions: 70 µm cell
 > strainer

 - Pre-wetted the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, wash the stainer with 5 ml media, and discard the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- RBC lysis: 4 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 20 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 2 ml of DMEM/F12 media and made a 1:10 dilution for
 > counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry, and freezing.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).
 > Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - dispase dissociation media: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 200 µl dispase
 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

rotating).
 > Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filter the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions. **Keep the
 > tissue leftovers.**

- **For cell suspension (dispase only):**

 - RBC lysis: 4 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 20 ml cold
 > DMEM/F12+++ media, centrifuged

 - Resuspended in 2 ml of DMEM/F12 media and made a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for
 > flow cytometry, long-term organoid establishment, and

- Further, the leftover tissue was dissociated with C&H for 1 h at

 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 2 ml of DMEM/F12 media and made a 1:10 dilution for

3\. \*Fibroblast enrichment\*:

Rest of dispase + C&H dissociated cells was used for fibroblast
enrichment. The total cell count was 42.2 E6 cells before starting the
protocol.

Anti-fibroblast MicroBeads protocol by Miltenyi Biotec was used as
guidance.

Used the previously prepared MACS buffer, but re- sterile-filtered
before using it.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice when not needed inside
the hood.

Cells were centrifuged at 300 g for 5 min RT and the cell pellet was
resuspended in 80 µl of cold MACS buffer (Commercial protocol suggests
80 µl per 10 E7 cells. To the suspension, 20 µl of Anti-Fibroblast
MicroBeads was added (protocol: 20 µl per 10 E7 cells) Cell suspension
was mixed well and incubated 30 min at RT. Cells were washed by adding 1
ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 300 g for 10 min. The supernatant was
aspirated and cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

ed 30 min at RT. Cells were washed by adding 1
ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 300 g for 10 min. The supernatant was
aspirated and cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

LS column and 30 µm filter were placed in the magnetic MACS Separator.
Column and filter were prepared by washing it with 3 ml MACS buffer. The
cell suspension was added to the column and washed with 3 x 3 ml MACS
buffer. Collected the flow-through to a 15 ml falcon tube which should
include all cells except fibroblasts. The column was removed from the
separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was
added to the column and with firmly pushing the plunger into the column,
magnetically labeled cells flushed out. Both cell suspensions were
centrifuged 300 g for 10 min, and resuspended in 500 µl of DMEM/F12+++
media.

nd an additional 10 min at RT shielded
 > from light.

- After incubation, washed once with 900 µl FACS buffer.

 > cells and incubated for 40 min at +4°C shielded from light.

- After incubation, 1 ml 1x Permeabilization buffer, and incubated 5
 > min at +4°C.

- Centrifuged, washed the pellet with 1 ml 1x Permeabilization buffer,
 > and resuspended in 900 µl FACS buffer for overnight storage.

- Next day, centrifuged, diluted intracellular antibody in
 > Permeabilization buffer, and resuspended pellet into 50 µl
 > antibody mix. Incubate 30 min at +4°C and an additional 10 min at
 > RT shielded from light.

- Washed once with 1 ml 1x Permeabilization buffer and resuspended in
 > 400 µl FACS buffer.

- Pipeted the cell suspension through 5 ml Falcon polystyrene tube
 > with a cell-strainer cap (blue).

# Sample S484_iOme 

**02.09.2024 - day 0 and 03.09.2024 - day 1**

**Copied from Dissociation_protocol_optimization_S484_iOme:**

Approaches:

1\. \*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples

- Minced the tumor piece into small pieces using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

r samples

- Minced the tumor piece into small pieces using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated sample with C&H for 1 hour at +37°C rotating. Vortexed
 > before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions: 70 µm cell
 > strainer

 - Pre-wetted the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, wash the stainer with 5 ml media, and discard the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- RBC lysis: 2 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 10 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media and made a 1:10 dilution for
 > counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).
 > Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - dispase dissociation media: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 200 µl dispase
 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filter the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions. **Keep the

dispase dissociation media: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 200 µl dispase
 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filter the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions. **Keep the
 > tissue leftovers.**

- **For cell suspension (dispase only):**

 - RBC lysis: 2 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 10 ml cold
 > DMEM/F12+++ media, centrifuged

 - Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media. Counted the cells and
 > prepared the aliquots for flow cytometry, long-term organoid
 > establishment, and freezing.

- Further, the leftover tissue was dissociated with C&H for 1 h at
 > +37°C rotating. Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media 4.45 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 500 µl C&H
 > (10x stock) + 50 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions

- RBC lysis: 2 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 10 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media and made a 1:10 dilution for
 > counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filter the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions. **Keep the
 > tissue leftovers.**

- **For cell suspension (dispase only):**

 - RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold
 > DMEM/F12+++ media, centrifuged

 - Resuspended in 2 ml of DMEM/F12 media, and prepared a 1:10
 > dilution for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the
 > aliquots for flow cytometry, and freezing.

- Further, the leftover tissue was dissociated with C&H for 1 h at
 > +37°C rotating. Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions

- RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 2 ml of DMEM/F12 media, and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

- Washed cells once with 900 µl FACS buffer (centrifuge 6min 400 g at
 > +4°C).

- For multicolor samples, diluted the antibodies in Brilliant stain
 > buffer (#563794 BD Horizon) (see calculations above), added 50 µl
 > of the antibody mix to each sample, and incubated 30 min at RT
 > shielded from light.

- For FMO samples, added the needed amount Brilliant Stain buffer (see
 > table above) to the cells and then added antibodies one by one.
 > Incubated 30 min at RT shielded from light.

- After incubation, washed once with 900 µl FACS buffer.

- Added 200 µl IC Fixation buffer (#00-8222 Invitrogen) to fix the
 > cells and incubated for 40 min at +4°C shielded from light.

- After incubation, 1 ml 1x Permeabilization buffer, and incubated 5
 > min at +4°C.

- Centrifuged, washed the pellet with 1 ml 1x Permeabilization buffer,

shielded from light.

- After incubation, 1 ml 1x Permeabilization buffer, and incubated 5
 > min at +4°C.

- Centrifuged, washed the pellet with 1 ml 1x Permeabilization buffer,
 > and resuspended in 900 µl FACS buffer for overnight storage.

- Next day, centrifuged, diluted intracellular antibodies in
 > Permeabilization buffer, and resuspended into 50 µl antibody mix
 > (all multicolor samples and from FMO samples all except a-SMA and
 > PDGFRb). For a-SMA and PDGFRb FMO samples, added first the needed
 > amount of Permeabilization buffer and then the needed antibodies
 > one by one. Incubate 30 min at RT shielded from light.

- Washed once with 1 ml 1x Permeabilization buffer and resuspended in
 > 400 µl FACS buffer.

- Pipeted the cell suspension through 5 ml Falcon polystyrene tube
 > with a cell-strainer cap (blue).

Preparation of compensation controls

- Used BD CompBeads Plus (7.5 µm) compensation beads.

- Added one drop of both positive (BD CompBeads AntiMouse IgG,
 > #51-9006274) and negative (CompBeads Negative Control #51-9006227)
 > compensation beads to 5ml Falcon polystyrene round bottom tubes
 > (white cap).

- For stained compensation controls, added 2 µl of each antibody.

 - the same antibodies except CD16-BV510 instead of BV510-DCM.

 - for aSMA, 2.5 µl instead of 2 µl

- Incubated approx. 1 hour at +4°C shielded from light and resuspended

ibody.

# 19.-20.09.2024

Went though all flow data so far that is included into dissociation
optimizations i.e. S470_pOme, S473_pOme, S481_pAdnR, S484_iOme, and
S488_iOme. From each sample, exported an excel file containing
frequencies of each cell population and combined all in one excel sheet
"Dissociation protocol optimization freqs".

Samples S470_pOme and S473_pOme were dissociated with 6 different
dissociation methods so those sample will be excluded form the final
analysis. Now when testing the analysis methods, included those.

on: phenotyping and

All plates were in a confluence of \~90%. First detached one plate but

plates.

plates and 2.5/8 in two plates → 3x S488_iOme P3 (⅛ P2) after dispase +
C&H, D/F+++ +10% FBS, and 2x S488_iOme P3 (2.5/8 P2) after dispase +
C&H, D/F+++ +10% FBS

and incubated 2 min at +37°C. Added PBS to the plate and transferred the
cells in a falcon tube. Centrifuged 300 g 5 min and respended the cells
in 1 ml DMEM/F12+++ +10%FBS. Counted the cells:

# 4.10.2024

**S051_pOme P16 (⅓ P15, 23.9.2024 JJ) → S051_pOme P17 (⅓ P16, 4.10.2024
AE)**

Splitted the organoids according to the protocol "Short format organoid
passaging protocol".

Splitted 2 wells into 3 wells each, and discarded the 3rd well. In total
6 wells.

While splitting, kept the organoids from 2 different wells separately.
Prepared also 2 vials with diluted Cultrex: to achieve 7.5 mg/ml
concentration of Cultrex, diluted 481 µl of stock (11.7 mg/ml) into 269
µl cold DPBS. Both diluted Cultrex solutions should have contained over
750 µl but when pipetting took everything (which was under 750 µl) and
added that into the cell pellet. Made 3 x 10 droplets of the first
detached well and 3 x 11 droplets of the second well. The pellet of
second well was big compared to the one from first.

Incubated the droplets for 45 min and in meantime prepared 19 ml of M1
media with 9.5 µl ROCKinhibitor and warmed that. Lastly, added 3 ml of
media to each well.

Experiment: Dissociation protocol optimization **S491_pOme**

Objective: Optimize the dissociation protocol to isolate more
fibroblasts from ovarian tumors.

Approaches:

1\. \*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples

- Washed big tissue pieces twice with HBSS.

- Minced the tumor piece into small pieces using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated sample with C&H for 1 hour at +37°C rotating. Vortexed
 > before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions: 70 µm cell
 > strainer

 - Pre-wetted the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, wash the stainer with 5 ml media, and discard the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 2 ml of DMEM/F12 media and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).

nted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).
 > Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - dispase dissociation media: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 200 µl dispase
 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filter the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions. **Keep the
 > tissue leftovers.**

- **For cell suspension (dispase only):**

 - RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold
 > DMEM/F12+++ media, centrifuged

 - Resuspended in 2 ml of DMEM/F12 media, and prepared a 1:10
 > dilution for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the
 > aliquots for flow cytometry, and freezing.

- Further, the leftover tissue was dissociated with C&H for 1 h at
 > +37°C rotating. Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions

- RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 2 ml of DMEM/F12 media, and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

Experiment: Dissociation protocol optimization **S493_iOme**

Objective: Optimize the dissociation protocol to isolate more
fibroblasts from ovarian tumors.

Approaches:

1\. \*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples

- Washed big tissue pieces twice with HBSS.

- Minced the tumor piece into small pieces using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated sample with C&H for 1 hour at +37°C rotating. Vortexed
 > before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions: 70 µm cell
 > strainer

 - Pre-wetted the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, wash the stainer with 5 ml media, and discard the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, wash the stainer with 5 ml media, and discard the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- RBC lysis: 2 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 10 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).
 > Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - dispase dissociation media: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 200 µl dispase
 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filter the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions. **Keep the
 > tissue leftovers.**

- **For cell suspension (dispase only):**

 - RBC lysis: 2 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 10 ml cold
 > DMEM/F12+++ media, centrifuged

 - Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media, and prepared a 1:10
 > dilution for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the
 > aliquots for flow cytometry, and freezing.

- Further, the leftover tissue was dissociated with C&H for 1 h at
 > +37°C rotating. Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x

tissue was dissociated with C&H for 1 h at

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions

- RBC lysis: 2 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 10 ml cold DMEM/F12+++

- Resuspended in 2 ml of DMEM/F12 media, and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow

After the 45 min but before adding the media, could clearly see cells as
a pellet in all the centrifuged wells. In addition, the cells in media
that was not centifuged were at the bottom of the u-bottom. However,
after I added the media, the cells "disappeared" meaning that they were
not clearly in the bottom of the well and hard to focus on. The media
was added really carefully along the well wall, so the cells and the
matrix (Cultrex) are definitely in the well.

Suggestion for next time: Cultrex could be diluted with media so there
is no need to add media on top of the droplets.

Next day - 23.10.2024 took pictures of the wells: Cells without matrix
were easy to find and clearly in the bottom of the wells. However, cells
in Cultrex were still hard to find in most of the wells. Especially in
wells with cells in Cultrex 7.5 mg/ml and centrifuged, cells appeared to
be in the top parts of the well as the microscopy could not focus high
enough.

(d0, d5 and d7) but due to issues in cell culture room, I was able to
again take pictures today on 29.10. d13. However, since the organoids
had changed so much between the d7 and d13, I managed to find very few
of the specific spots of spheroids, which I had been following. Took

Splitted in accordance to the Short format organoid passaging protocol.

**30.10.2024 - day 0 and 31.10.2024 - day 1**

**Copied from Dissociation_protocol_optimization**

Experiment: Dissociation protocol optimization **S494_pOme**

Objective: Optimize the dissociation protocol to isolate more
fibroblasts from ovarian tumors.

S494_pOme sample was processed together with S495_pOme

Approaches:

1\. \*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\.

*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples

- Washed big tissue pieces once with HBSS.

- Minced the tumor piece into small pieces using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated sample with C&H for 1 hour at +37°C rotating. Vortexed
 > before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions: 70 µm cell
 > strainer

 - Pre-wetted the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, wash the stainer with 5 ml media, and discard the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

f DMEM/F12 media and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).
 > Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - dispase dissociation media: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 200 µl dispase
 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filter the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions. **Keep the
 > tissue leftovers.**

- **For cell suspension (dispase only):**

 - Centrifuged the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

 - Then, it was resuspended in 1 ml media to wait for the other
 > conditions to be at the same step. Then, centrifuged again,
 > and continued with RBC lysis.

 - RBC lysis: 2 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 10 ml cold
 > DMEM/F12+++ media, centrifuged

 - Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media, and prepared a 1:10
 > dilution for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the
 > aliquots for flow cytometry, and freezing.

- Further, the leftover tissue was dissociated with C&H for 1 h at
 > +37°C rotating. Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions

- RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold DMEM/F12+++

1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions

- RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media, and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples

- Washed big tissue pieces once with HBSS.

- Minced the tumor piece into small pieces using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated sample with C&H for 1 hour at +37°C rotating. Vortexed
 > before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions: 70 µm cell
 > strainer

 - Pre-wetted the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, wash the stainer with 5 ml media, and discard the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).

nted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).
 > Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - dispase dissociation media: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 200 µl dispase
 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filter the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions. **Keep the
 > tissue leftovers.**

- **For cell suspension (dispase only):**

 - Centrifuged the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

 - Then, it was resuspended in 1 ml media to wait for the other
 > conditions to be at the same step. Then, centrifuged again,
 > and continued with RBC lysis.

 - RBC lysis: 2 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 10 ml cold
 > DMEM/F12+++ media, centrifuged

 - Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media, and prepared a 1:10
 > dilution for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the
 > aliquots for flow cytometry, and freezing.

- Further, the leftover tissue was dissociated with C&H for 1 h at
 > +37°C rotating. Vortexed before and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions

- RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

 > antibodies were diluted in Brilliant stain buffer (#563794 BD
 > Horizon) (see calculations above), 50 µl of the antibody mix was
 > added to each sample, and the samples were incubated for 30 min at
 > RT shielded from light.

- For the remaining FMO samples, the cells were added to the needed
 > amount of Brilliant Stain buffer (see table above), and then the
 > antibodies were added one by one. They were incubated for 30 min
 > at RT, and shielded from light.

- After incubation, washed once with 900 µl FACS buffer.

- Added 200 µl IC Fixation buffer (#00-8222 Invitrogen) to fix the
 > cells and incubated for 40 min at +4°C shielded from light.

- After incubation, 1 ml 1x Permeabilization buffer, and incubated for
 > 5 min at +4°C.

- Centrifuged, washed the pellet with 1 ml 1x Permeabilization buffer,
 > and resuspended in 900 µl FACS buffer for overnight storage.

- Next day, centrifuged, diluted intracellular antibodies in

shed the pellet with 1 ml 1x Permeabilization buffer,

 > (white cap).

# Sample S503_pAdnR

### diagnosis: ca serosum high grade, ca clarocellulare (mixed)

**18.11.2024 - day 0 and 19.11.2024 - day 1**

**Copied from Dissociation_protocol_optimization:**

Experiment: Dissociation protocol optimization **S503_pAdnR**

Objective: Optimize the dissociation protocol to isolate more
fibroblasts from ovarian tumors.

Approaches:

1\. \*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

ors.

Approaches:

1\. \*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor samples. I got only a small piece that was
 > already cut into halves.

- Minced the tumor piece into small pieces using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated sample with C&H for 1 hour at +37°C rotating. Vortexed
 > before, in the middle, and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions: 70 µm cell
 > strainer

 - Pre-wetted the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transfer the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, wash the stainer with 5 ml media, and discard the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- RBC lysis: 2 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 10 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).
 > Vortexed before, in the middle, and after the incubation period.

 - dispase dissociation media: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 200 µl dispase
 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filter the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions. **Keep the
 > tissue leftovers.**

- **For cell suspension (dispase only):**

 - Centrifuged the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

 - RBC lysis: 3 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 15 ml cold
 > DMEM/F12+++ media, centrifuged

 - Resuspended in 2 ml of DMEM/F12 media, and prepared a 1:10
 > dilution for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the
 > aliquots for flow cytometry, and freezing.

- Further, the leftover tissue was dissociated with C&H for 1 h at
 > +37°C rotating. Vortexed before, in the middle, and after the
 > incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions

- RBC lysis: 1 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 10 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media.

- Resuspended in 1 ml of DMEM/F12 media. Counted the cells and

 > to the wells, and the cells were not resuspended in the media/gel
 > solution. In addition, (1) the plate was kept cold so that the
 > Cultrex droplets did not start solidifying until after the
 > centrifugation, and (2) fibrinogen was added carefully to the
 > wells after centrifugation so that the ome-gels/fibrin matrices
 > didn't either solidify before centrifugation.

- Cells in omentum = omentum diluted in media

- Cells in "normal" ome-gel = ome-gel prepared as previously

 > fibrinogen, aprotinin, and thrombin was increased by x2.12

 > fibrinogen, aprotinin, and thrombin was increased by x5

- Cells in 1.06 mg/ml Cultrex = Cultrex diluted in media (NOTE!
 > Cultrex was previously diluted in DPBS)

- Cells in 2.5 mg/ml Cultrex = Cultrex diluted in media

- Cells in 7.5 mg/ml Cultrex = Cultrex diluted in media

 > thrombin as in the original ome-gel, but diluted in media (no

 > each of the 3 different cell solutions.

 > different cell solutions.

 > supernatant. Keep the cells on ice.

1. Prepare Cultrex droplets

 a. Add 60 µL of diluted 10 % Cultrex solution to one with each cell
 > concentration and mix gently.

 > cell suspension. Keep the remaining cell suspension with

 > droplet into the corresponding wells from each cell
 > suspension.

 > on ice.

**U-bottom plate:**

Media was changed on day2, day4, day6, and day8. Removed as much media
as possible, at least half and added same amount on top. For suspension
cells, removed and added around 50 µl and for cultrex/ome-gel/fibrin,
removed and added 150 µl. For cells in ometum+media solution, prepared a
fresh solution and changed that.

On day 8 (11.12.2024) pictures were taken before and after centrifuging
the plate. Centifugation was done so see if the content of the wells is
easier to image after that. Tried centrifugation with a short spin for
15s, 20s, 30s, and 40s, spin reched maximum of 2250 g. After that tried
200 g for 3 min and 300 g 5 min. Took pictures whenever something
changed in the well/was able to see the cells better. Pictures named
...\_111224_a_AE were taken before centrifugation and then
...\_111224_b_AE after 15s short spin, ...\_111224_c_AE after 20s short
spin, ...\_111224_d_AE after 40s short spin, and ...\_111224_f_AE after
300 g 5min.

plate. Those images are named as ...\_131224_b_AE.

On day 10, decided to fix the cells. Fixed all wells except "cells in
omentum" and "cells in fibrin matrix" as there were no clear cell
spheroid/gel structure that wasn't attached to bottom of the well.

**Copied from Dissociation_protocol_optimization_S506_pAdnR:**

Experiment: Dissociation protocol optimization **S506_pAdnR**

Objective: Optimize the dissociation protocol to isolate more
fibroblasts from ovarian tumors.

Approaches:

1\.

:**

Experiment: Dissociation protocol optimization **S506_pAdnR**

Objective: Optimize the dissociation protocol to isolate more
fibroblasts from ovarian tumors.

Approaches:

1\. \*Using trypsin and collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

1 hour C&H

2\. \*Using dispase followed by collagenase & hyaluronidase\*:

30 min dispase + 1 hour C&H

Experimental Steps:

1\. \*Sample Preparation\*:

- Collected ovarian tumor sample. The piece was huge. Used ⅓ of the
 > whole piece, specifically the part closest to the part where
 > Anastasia took tissue pieces.

- Minced the tumor piece into small pieces using a sterile scalpel and
 > scissors, and divided the minced tissue into two equal parts.

2\. \*Dissociation Protocols\*:

\*Collagenase & hyaluronidase-only conditions\*:

- Treated sample with C&H for 1 hour at +37°C rotating. Vortexed
 > before, in the middle, and after the incubation period.

 - C&H dissociation media: 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ media + 1 ml C&H (10x
 > stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filtered the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions: 70 µm cell
 > strainer

 - Pre-wetted the filter with 1 ml DMEM/F12+++, transferred the
 > digested suspension through the strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, washed the stainer with 5 ml media, and discarded the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

strainer into a 50 ml Falcon
 > tube, washed the stainer with 5 ml media, and discarded the
 > leftover tissue pieces.

 - Centrifuge the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

- RBC lysis: 5 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 20 ml cold DMEM/F12+++
 > media, centrifuged

- Resuspended in 10 ml of DMEM/F12 media and prepared a 1:10 dilution
 > for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the aliquots for flow
 > cytometry.

\*Dispase followed by collagenase conditions\*:

- Treated sample with dispase for 30 minutes (at +37°C rotating).
 > Vortexed before, in the middle, and after the incubation period.

 - dispase dissociation media: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 200 µl dispase
 > 50 (U/ml stock) + 100 µl DNAse (RNAse free)

- Filter the samples to obtain single-cell suspensions. **Keep the
 > tissue leftovers.**

- **For cell suspension (dispase only):**

 - Centrifuged the cell suspension 10 min 1000 rpm at +4°C

 - RBC lysis: 5 ml, incubated 5 min RT, added 20 ml cold
 > DMEM/F12+++ media, centrifuged

 - Resuspended in 10 ml of DMEM/F12 media, and prepared a 1:10
 > dilution for counting. Counted the cells and prepared the
 > aliquots for flow cytometry, and freezing.

- Further, the leftover tissue was dissociated with C&H for 1 h at
 > +37°C rotating. Vortexed before, in the middle, and after the
 > incubation period.

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated first using
Dispase, followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H). Dispase
dissociation media included 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented
with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 200 µl Dispase + 100
µl DNAse, and the sample was incubated at 37C rotating for 30 min,
vortexed before and after. Suspension was filtered through 70 µm cell
strainer.

The leftover pieces were further dissociated with C&H. The C&H
dissociation media included 8.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ + 1 ml C&H+ 100 µl DNAse.
The sample was incubated at 37C rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and
after. After incubation with C&H, the suspension was filtered through a
70 µm cell strainer.

For both cell suspensions (dispase dissociated and dispase+C&H
dissociated) RBC lysis with 2 ml for 5 min + 10 ml DMEM/F12+++, and the
final pellet was resuspended in 1 ml each. From both, a 1:10 dilution
was made for counting.

From dispase+C&H dissociated cells, took 1.5 E6 cells aside, and
continued to MACS with the rest (12.9 E6 cells).

A) **Magnetic-activated cell sorting (MACS)**

Anti-fibroblast MicroBeads protocol by Miltenyi Biotec was used as
guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and
CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA) to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in
DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered before using
it.

Centrifuged the cells at 300 g for 10 min at +4C. The pellet was
resuspended in 103.2 µl of cold MACS buffer (commercial protocol
suggests 80 µl per 10 E6 cells). Added 25.8 µl of Anti-Fibroblast
MicroBeads to the cell suspension (protocol: 20 µl per 10 E6 cells) and
incubated 30 min at RT. Washed the cells with 2 ml MACS buffer and
centrifuged 300 g for 10 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended
in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice. The beads were kept
sterile and therefore worked inside a laminar hood until the incubation.

LS column and 30 µm filter were placed in the magnetic MACS Separator.

e kept on ice. The beads were kept
sterile and therefore worked inside a laminar hood until the incubation.

LS column and 30 µm filter were placed in the magnetic MACS Separator.
Prepared them by washing them with 3 ml of MACS buffer, then applied the
cell suspension onto the column, and lastly, washed with 3 x 3 ml MACS
buffer.

Always wait until all liquid has entered through the column.

The column was removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml
falcon tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and with firmly
pushing the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells were
flushed out.

Both cell suspensions were centrifuged 300 x for 10 min and then
resuspended in 1 ml FACS buffer. Counted the cells, and cell counts:

Anti-fibroblast MicroBeads, CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads, and CD45
MicroBeads protocols by Miltenyi Biotec were used as guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and
CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in
DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered before using
it.

Divided the 33.45 E6 cells by two to have 16.725 E6 cells for both MACS
protocols. Centrifuged the cells at 300 g for 10 min at +4C.

Using anti-fibroblast beads: The pellet was resuspended in 133.8 µl of
cold MACS buffer (commercial protocol suggests 80 µl per 10 E6 cells).
Added 33.45 µl of Anti-Fibroblast MicroBeads to the cell suspension
(protocol: 20 µl per 10 E6 cells) and incubated 30 min at RT shielded
from light.

ggests 80 µl per 10 E6 cells).

(protocol: 20 µl per 10 E6 cells) and incubated 30 min at RT shielded
from light. Washed the cells with 4 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 300 g
for 10 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS
buffer.

Using negative isolation: The pellet was resuspended in 433,1 µl of cold
MACS buffer (calculation: the desired volume \[500 µl\] - the volume of
both beads \[2x 33.54 µl\]). Added 33.45 µl of both anti-EpCAM and
anti-CD45 beads to the cell suspension and incubated 30 min at +4C
shielded from light. Washed the cells with 4 ml MACS buffer and
centrifuged 300 g for 10 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended
in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice. The beads were kept
sterile and, therefore, worked inside a laminar hood until incubation.

3 ml MACS buffer.

d has entered through the column.

All cell suspensions were centrifuged 300 x for 10 min and then

**10.02.2025**

**Copied from patient sample procotol:**

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase,
followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H).

Dispase dissociation media included 19.4 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12
supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 400 µl
Dispase + 200 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37C rotating for 30 min, vortexed
before and after. Filtered through 70 µm cell strainer.

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 17.8 ml DMEM/F12+++,
2 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 200 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37C
rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm
cell strainer.

RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++ for dispase
dissociated and with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++ for sequentially
dissociated.

hrough a 70 µm
cell strainer.

dissociated and with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++ for sequentially
dissociated.

After RBC lysis and centrifugation, the sequentially dissociated cells
looked slightly pink, so redid RBC lysis with 2 ml + 10 ml DMEM/F12+++.

The final pellet was resuspended in 8 ml or 2 ml of media (dispase and
sequential respectively). For counting, a 1:10 dilution of the cell
suspension was made.

B) **Magnetic-activated cell sorting (MACS)**

Performed negative isolation for all obtained sequentially dissociatated
cells. Cell count was 142.8 E6, and divided that into 3 equal fractions.
Made the calculations assuming 50 E6 cells (in 3 separate fractions).

**Negative isolation:** Depleting the sample from cancer and immune
cells using a mixture of anti-EpCAM (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-061-101) and
anti-CD45 (TIL) beads (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-118-780), and an LS column in
a QuadroMACS separator.

CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads, and CD45 MicroBeads protocols by Miltenyi
Biotec were used as guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and
CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in
DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered before using
it.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice.

Everything was done inside a laminar hood to keep the cells sterile.

Centrifuged the cells at 300 g for 10 min at +4C.

The pellets were resuspended in 300 µl of cold MACS buffer (calculation:
the desired volume \[500 µl\] - the volume of both beads \[2x 100 µl\]).

ells at 300 g for 10 min at +4C.

the desired volume \[500 µl\] - the volume of both beads \[2x 100 µl\]).
Added 100 µl of both anti-EpCAM and anti-CD45 beads to the cell
suspension and incubated 30 min at +4C shielded from light.

Washed the cells with 3 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 1500 rpm (408 g)
for 5 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS
buffer.

Placed three LS columns and 30 µm filters in the magnetic MACS
Separator. Prepared them by washing them with 3 ml of MACS buffer, then
applied the cell suspension onto the column, and washed with 3 x 3 ml
MACS buffer.

Columns were removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon
tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and with firmly pushing
the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells were flushed
out.

All cell suspensions were centrifuged 1500 rpm g for 5 min. Resuspended
the flowthought cells in 1 ml iPDC media and the column-bound fracton in
2 ml DMEM/F12+++ media. Counted the cells.

## Cell harvesting timepoints

## Summarized results

![](media/image103.png){width="6.5in" height="3.0in"}

Cell viability was dramatically decreasing over time and, because of
this, the experiment was terminated at d7 instead of d3. For the next
experiment, the plating density will be adjusted to 1000 **LIVE**
cells/uL.

Also, all endothelial cells were already dead at d3, so for the next
experiment a positive isolation purification (with anti-fibroblast
beads) will be tested to exclude endothelial cells from the cultures and
perhaps enhance the viability. -\> In a meeting with Anniina this was
disregarded. Anniina does not think it's a big problem to have few
endothelial cells dying in the cultures.

On the other hand, the cancer cell population also seemed to be
declining, however it might be because these cells are forming clumps
that are excluded from the flow analysis (through filtering, and doublet
exclusion). For the next experiment, I will try to take pictures using
the Evos microscope in the cell culture room. Also, it would be nice to
try to count the cells and assess the viability with the cell counter
too.

## Cell harvesting timepoints

Overview: spheroids from two different wells were imaged daily using the
EVOS XL Core in the cell culture room. Images from all the available
magnifications were saved (4x, 10x, 20x, and 40x). Cells were harvested
as described below on days 3, 7, and 9, and stained for flow cytometry.
Media was changed twice during the course of 9 days. **Perhaps it would
have been better to change it thrice (every 3 days)**

# ![](media/image8.png){width="6.5in" height="3.263888888888889in"}

To produce these plots, flow cytometry data was first analyzed using
FlowJo. After compensation, gates for time, cells, and live cells were
drawn to: 1) exclude bad quality data, 2) exclude debris, and 3) exclude
dead cells. Next, a CD45 vs EpCAM plot was generated, and the three
resulting populations were used for the "general cell types" plots:

- (CD45+EpCAM- (immune cells)

- CD45-EpCAM+ (cancer cells)

- CD45-EpCAM-(non-immune stromal cells).

For the "specific cell types" plots, the CD45+EpCAM- population was
selected, and a CD8 vs CD3 plot was generated. The three resulting
subpopulations were:

- CD3+CD8- (Non-CD8+ T cells, possibly CD4+ T cells)

- CD3+CD8+ (CD8+ T cells)

- CD3-CD8- (And other immune cells, possibly myeloid cells).

Additionally, the CD45-EpCAM- population was selected, and a histogram
of CD31 expression was plotted. The resulting populations were:

- CD31- (Possibly fibroblasts)

- CD31+ (endothelial cells).

Finally, the percentage of each population (out of all cells in the live
gate) was calculated and saved in an Excel file named
**"S530_pOme_cell_percentages_16042025_MH"**, located in the P Drive:

ntage of each population (out of all cells in the live
gate) was calculated and saved in an Excel file named

B) **Magnetic-activated cell sorting (MACS)**

Performed negative isolation for all obtained sequentially dissociatated
cells. Cell count was 183.8 E6, and divided that into 3 equal fractions.
Made the calculations assuming 60 E6 cells in 3 separate fractions.

**Negative isolation:** Depleting the sample from cancer and immune
cells using a mixture of anti-EpCAM (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-061-101) and
anti-CD45 (TIL) beads (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-118-780), and an LS column in
a QuadroMACS separator.

er and immune
cells using a mixture of anti-EpCAM (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-061-101) and
anti-CD45 (TIL) beads (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-118-780), and an LS column in
a QuadroMACS separator.

CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads, and CD45 MicroBeads protocols by Miltenyi
Biotec were used as guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and
CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in
DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered before using
it.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice.

Everything was done inside a laminar hood to keep the cells sterile.

Centrifuged the cells at 300 g for 10 min at +4C.

The pellets were resuspended in 260 µl of cold MACS buffer (calculation:
the desired volume \[500 µl\] - the volume of both beads \[2x 120 µl\]).
Added 120 µl of both anti-EpCAM and anti-CD45 beads to the cell
suspension and incubated 30 min at +4C shielded from light.

Washed the cells with 2 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 1500 rpm (408 g)
for 5 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS
buffer.

Placed three LS columns and 30 µm filters in the magnetic MACS
Separator. Prepared them by washing them with 3 ml of MACS buffer, then
applied the cell suspension onto the column, and washed with 3 x 3 ml
MACS buffer.

Columns were removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon
tube.

MACS buffer, then
applied the cell suspension onto the column, and washed with 3 x 3 ml
MACS buffer.

Columns were removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon
tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and with firmly pushing
the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells were flushed
out.

All cell suspensions were centrifuged 1500 rpm g for 5 min. Resuspended
the flowthought cells in 2 ml iPDC media and the column-bound fraction
in 4 ml DMEM/F12+++ media. Counted the cells.
