Mg2+). Then, 3.5 mL of pre-warmed dispase II solution were added to each
well (14.55 mL of DMEM/F12 + 300 uL Dispase II (50X) + 150 uL RNAse-free
DNase), and before moving the plates back into the incubator, the
droplets were detached using a cell scraper and the floating gels were
pipetted up and down with a P1000 4-5 times until the matrix was
disrupted. This was a modification compared to the previous protocol to
try to enhance the cell recovery. According to the long-term organoid
protocol, the gels must be disrupted in order for them to digest well.

To produce these plots, flow cytometry data was first analyzed using
FlowJo. After compensation, gates for time, cells, and live cells were
drawn to: 1) exclude bad quality data, 2) exclude debris, and 3) exclude
dead cells. Next, a CD45 vs EpCAM plot was generated, and the three
resulting populations were used for the "general cell types" plots:

- (CD45+EpCAM- (immune cells)

- CD45-EpCAM+ (cancer cells)

- CD45-EpCAM-(non-immune stromal cells).

For the "specific cell types" plots, the CD45+EpCAM- population was
selected, and a CD8 vs CD3 plot was generated. The three resulting
subpopulations were:

- CD3+CD8- (Non-CD8+ T cells, possibly CD4+ T cells)

- CD3+CD8+ (CD8+ T cells)

- CD3-CD8- (And other immune cells, possibly myeloid cells).

Additionally, the CD45-EpCAM- population was selected, and a histogram
of CD31 expression was plotted. The resulting populations were:

- CD31- (Possibly fibroblasts)

- CD31+ (endothelial cells).

Finally, the percentage of each population (out of all cells in the live

as plotted. The resulting populations were:

- CD31- (Possibly fibroblasts)

- CD31+ (endothelial cells).

Finally, the percentage of each population (out of all cells in the live
gate) was calculated and saved in an Excel file named
**"S540_pOme2_cell_percentages_03042025_MH"**, located in the P Drive:

**P:\\h345\\afarkkilab\\Projects\\iPDC_2.0\\TME_culture_optimization\\Flow_cytometry\\Raw_data_and_FlowJo_workspaces\\S540_pOme**

This is the input file to run the script to plot the proportions as
either bar, or line plots.

B) **Magnetic-activated cell sorting (MACS)**

Performed negative isolation for all sequentially dissociated cells.
Took 3x 60 E6 cells for 3 separate fractions.

**Negative isolation:** Depleting the sample from cancer and immune
cells using a mixture of anti-EpCAM (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-061-101) and

3x 60 E6 cells for 3 separate fractions.

**Negative isolation:** Depleting the sample from cancer and immune
cells using a mixture of anti-EpCAM (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-061-101) and
anti-CD45 (TIL) beads (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-118-780), and an LS column in
a QuadroMACS separator.

CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads, and CD45 MicroBeads protocols by Miltenyi
Biotec were used as guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and
CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in
DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered before being
used.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice.

Everything was done inside a laminar hood to keep the cells sterile.

Centrifuged the cells at 1500 rpm for 5 min at +4C.

The pellets were resuspended in 260 µl of cold MACS buffer (calculation:
the desired volume \[500 µl\] - the volume of both beads \[2x 120 µl\]).
Added 120 µl of both anti-EpCAM and anti-CD45 beads to the cell
suspension and incubated 30 min at +4C shielded from light.

Washed the cells with 2 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 1500 rpm (408 g)
for 5 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS
buffer.

After this, CD45 (TIL) microbeads cat no. 130-118-780 lot 5241203993
vial was used up.

Placed three LS columns and 30 µm filters in the magnetic MACS
Separator.

the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells were flushed
out.

B) **Magnetic-activated cell sorting (MACS)**

Performed negative isolation for all obtained sequentially dissociated
cells. Cell count was 31.2 E6. The calculations were made assuming 30 E6
cells.

**Negative isolation:** Depleting the sample from cancer and immune
cells using a mixture of anti-EpCAM (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-061-101) and
anti-CD45 (TIL) beads (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-118-780), and an LS column in
a QuadroMACS separator.

CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads, and CD45 MicroBeads protocols by Miltenyi
Biotec were used as guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and
CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in
DPBS.

Miltenyi
Biotec were used as guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: 49.55 ml DPBS (w/o MgCl2 and
CaCl2) + 250 µl FBS + 200 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM EDTA in
DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered before being
used.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice.

Everything was done inside a laminar hood to keep the cells sterile.

Centrifuged the cells at 1500 rpm (408 g) for 5 min at +4C.

The pellets were resuspended in 380 µl of cold MACS buffer (calculation:
the desired volume \[500 µl\] - the volume of both beads \[2x 60 µl\]).
Added 60 µl of both anti-EpCAM and anti-CD45 beads to the cell
suspension and incubated 30 min at +4C shielded from light.

Washed the cells with 2 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 1500 rpm for 5
min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended in 500 µl of MACS buffer.

Placed LS column and 30 µm filter in the magnetic MACS Separator.
Prepared it by washing it with 3 ml of MACS buffer, then applied the
cell suspension onto the column, and washed with 3 x 3 ml MACS buffer.

Columns were removed from the separator and placed in a new 15 ml falcon
tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and by firmly pushing the
plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells were flushed out.

All cell suspensions were centrifuged at 1500 rpm g for 5 min.
Resuspended the flow-through cells in 1 ml iPDC media and the

column, magnetically labeled cells were flushed out.

All cell suspensions were centrifuged at 1500 rpm g for 5 min.
Resuspended the flow-through cells in 1 ml iPDC media and the
column-bound fraction in 3 ml DMEM/F12+++ media. Counted the cells.

Calculations:

10 wells, with each 8 droplets

1 000 **live** cells/µL \* 40 µL = 40 000 cells/droplet

40 000 cells/droplet \* 8 droplets/well \* 11 wells (one extra) = **3
520 000 live cells are needed**

8 droplets/ well \* 11 wells \* 40 µL/ droplet = **3 520 µL are needed
in total**

However, since the omentum gel start polymerizing as soon as the
fibrinogen is added to the mixture, it is better to make **five (5)**
separate tubes each one of 704 µL (3520 µL/5) and not everything at once
in a single tube.

ng as soon as the
fibrinogen is added to the mixture, it is better to make **five (5)**
separate tubes each one of 704 µL (3520 µL/5) and not everything at once
in a single tube.

We want to keep a cell density of 1 000 live cells/µL, so the total
number of cells per tube that will be needed is:

1 000 live cells/µL \* 704 µL = 0.704 E6 live cells/tube

0.704 E6 cells / (3.75 E6 cells/mL) = 188 µL from the original cell
suspension

The amount of the remaining components per tube will be:

ORG full medium: (704 µL --361.88 µL) = 342.12 µL

Omentum gel: 155.58 µL

Aprotinin: 2.11 µL

Thrombin: 2.11 µL

Fibrinogen: 14.08 µL

Total (ome-gel+apro+thro+fib) = 173.88 µL

Total (+cells) = 173.88 µL + 188 µL = 361.88 µL

**Aims of the experiment:**

The droplets were allowed to solidify for 1 hour in the incubator and,
after that time, 3.5 mL of iPDC media + cytokines were added into each
well.

**Aims of the experiment:**

- To evaluate the effect of GolgiPlug (Brefeldin A) and GolgiStop
 > (Monensin) on intracellular cytokine retention following T cell
 > activation.

- To assess baseline (unstimulated) cytokine levels and distinguish
 > them from induced levels under different conditions.

**Experimental layout:**

![](media/image21.png){width="6.5in" height="4.416666666666667in"}

**Experimental description:**

- Primary cells were seeded into 6-well plates (Plate 1 and Plate 2)
 > as described above.

- After a few hours of incubation to allow settling, **ImmunoCult™
 > Human T Cell Activator** will be added to selected wells (as
 > indicated in the schematic) and incubated for a total of **24
 > hours**.

- For wells with transport inhibitors, either **GolgiPlug (Brefeldin
 > A)** or **GolgiStop (Monensin)** will be added at the **20-hour
 > mark** (i.e., for the final 4 hours of the activation period), in
 > order to block cytokine secretion and allow intracellular
 > accumulation.

> **ImmunoCult™ Human T Cell Activator:** Suggested amount is 25 uL/mL
> of cell suspension (1E6 cells). In this experiment, the amount will be
> adjusted based on the volume of media (3.5 mL/well), and not the

tivator:** Suggested amount is 25 uL/mL
> of cell suspension (1E6 cells). In this experiment, the amount will be
> adjusted based on the volume of media (3.5 mL/well), and not the
> amount of cells, so **the amount to be added will be 87.5 uL/well**
>
> **GolgiPlug:** Suggested amount is 1 uL/mL of cell suspension (1E6
> cells). In this experiment, the amount will be adjusted based on the
> volume of media, and not the amount of cells, so **the amount to be
> added will be 3.5 uL/well**
>
> **GolgiStop:** Suggested amount is 4 uL/6mL of cell suspension (1E6
> cells). In this experiment, the amount will be adjusted based on the
> volume of media, and not the amount of cells, so **the amount to be
> added will be 2.3 uL/well**

**3.11.2025 Dissociation:**

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase,
followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H) by María and Sarah.

Dispase dissociation media included 67.9 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12
supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 1.4 ml
Dispase + 700 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C rotating for 30 min, vortexed
before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 53.4 ml DMEM/F12+++,
6 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 600 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C,
rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm
cell strainer.

Dispase dissociated cells:

RBC lysis with 5 ml for 5 min + 20 ml DMEM/F12+++. RBC lysis was
repeated with 7 ml for 5 min + 30 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was
resuspended in 20 mL of icPDC media (150 mL of icPDC media was
prepared). For cell counting a 1:10 dilution was used.

Sequentially dissociated cells:

RBC lysis with 7 ml for 5 min + 25 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was
resuspended in 20 mL of DMEM/F12+++ media. For cell counting a 1:10
dilution was used.

sterile 1x PBS was prepared

•cells were washed once with 70 µl sterile 1x PBS

•20 µl of the 1:1000 dilution of live-dye stain was added per well

•384-well plate was spin down at 300g, RT for 5 min

plate

•cells were washed once with 70 µl sterile 1x PBS

•20 µl of the 1:1000 dilution of live-dye stain was added per well

•384-well plate was spin down at 300g, RT for 5 min

•After incubation, washed once with 70 µl sterile 1x PBS per well

•cells were fixed by adding 50 µl of 4% paraformaldehyde and incubated
for 40 min at RT, protected from light.

•cells were washed three times with 70 µl of 1x PBS

•After washing, 70 µl of 1x PBS was added to each well and the plate was
sealed with parafilm, covered with tin foil and stored in +4°C

# Sample S102, S134 and S190

#### Objective: LEGENDplex experiment is a bead-based immunoassay to quantify 13 human proteins out of the supernatant from an experiment done by Kursat for the paper Perez-Villatoro et al.

#### 20.11.2025

Conditioned media from the HLA-Dr blocking experiment was collected by
Kursat and frozen at -80 deg until the time of the assay.

On the assay day (20.11.2025), María thawed the samples on ice and

from the HLA-Dr blocking experiment was collected by
Kursat and frozen at -80 deg until the time of the assay.

On the assay day (20.11.2025), María thawed the samples on ice and
performed the assay following the manufacturer's recommended protocol,
assisted by Sarah.

Protocol can be found here:

[[https://www.biolegend.com/Files/Images/media_assets/pro_detail/datasheets/Hu-CD8-Panel-V02_FK.pdf]{.underline}](https://www.biolegend.com/Files/Images/media_assets/pro_detail/datasheets/Hu-CD8-Panel-V02_FK.pdf)

Samples were run in duplicates.

Data was generated using the Fortessa cytometer in the Flow core, on the
same day that the assay was performed.

Data was analyzed using the LegendPlex proprietary software and final
plots were generated using R scripts and averaged data among technical
replicates, and then among patients. During the data analysis, Anniina
informed María via Slack message that the pembro and untreated
conditions were mistakenly switched during the blocking experiment, so
**the labels for these two conditions were switched in the final plots
as per her instructions.**

Raw data, generated plots, and scripts to generate the plots can be
found in P Drive:
P:\\h345\\afarkkilab\\Projects\\NKI\\iPDCs\\MHCII_blockade_LEGENDplex_assay

lagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H) by Sarah.

resuspended in 10 mL of DMEM/F12+++ media.

[06.12.2025 Harvesting:]{.underline}

60 ml of DispaseII solution (1.2 mL DispaseII (stock 50 U/mL) + 600 µl
DNase in 58.2 mL DMEM F12 +++ media ) was prepared.

Medium from wells were aspired using a suction pump and 3.5mL of
DispaseII solution were added into each well. Ome-gel droplets were
break up by scrapping with a cell scrapper and pipetting up and down
several times using a 1 mL pipet before the plate were incubated in cell
culture incubator for 30 min.

Each well was Transfer into 15 mL falcons and washed with 3.5 mL DMEM
F12 +++ media using the 1 mL pipet and additional 3.5 mL DMEM F12 +++
media were added to each falcon. Plates were checked in the microscope,
if still a considerable amount of cells are in the wells, but all were
clean.

All Falcons were centrifugated at 400g for 6 min at +4, supernatant was
removed pellets were resuspended in 800 µl FACS buffer and Falcons of
the same conditions were pooled.

**15.12.2025 Dissociation:**

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase,
followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H) by Sarah.

Dispase dissociation media included 38.8 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12
supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 800 µl
Dispase + 400 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C rotating for 30 min, vortexed

cluded 38.8 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12
supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 800 µl
Dispase + 400 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C rotating for 30 min, vortexed
before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 35.6 ml DMEM/F12+++,
4 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 400 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C,
rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm
cell strainer.

Dispase dissociated cells:

First RBC lysis with 3 ml for 5 min + 12 ml DMEM/F12+++. Second RBC
lysis with 1 ml for 5 min + 6 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was
resuspended in 10 mL of DMEM/F12+++ media.

Sequentially dissociated cells: RBC lysis with 3 ml for 5 min + 12 ml
DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was resuspended in 5 mL of DMEM/F12+++
media.

**Negative Isolation**

Performed negative isolation for the obtained sequentially dissociated
cells.

**Negative isolation:** Depleting the sample from cancer and immune
cells using a mixture of anti-EpCAM (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-061-101) and
anti-CD45 (TIL) beads (Miltenyi, Cat: 130-118-780), and an LS column in
a QuadroMACS separator.

CD326 (EpCAM) MicroBeads, and CD45 MicroBeads protocols by Miltenyi
Biotec were used as guidance.

MACS buffer was prepared as follows: (29,73 x 5 = 148,65 )ml DPBS (w/o
MgCl2 and CaCl2) + 750 µl FBS + 600 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM
EDTA in DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered
before using it.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice.

FBS + 600 µl EDTA to have 0.5 % FBS and 2 mM
EDTA in DPBS. MACS buffer was prepared sterile and sterile-filtered
before using it.

MACS buffer, cells, and beads were kept on ice.

Everything was done inside a laminar hood to keep the cells sterile.

Based on the cell count, divide the cell suspension into equal fractions
so that the maximum number of cells does not exceed the recommended
sample size. Recommended sample size for leukocytes: 0.1 E6 - 100 E6
labeled cells in 10 E6 - 2000 E6 total cells per column. Four (4) equal
fractions were calculated, requiring 2.208 µl of cold MACS buffer for a
total of 240E6 cells, specifically 60E6 cells per column.

Centrifuged the cells at 300 g for 10 min at +4C.

The pellets were resuspended in 2.208 µl of cold MACS buffer
(calculation: the desired volume \[500 µl\] - the volume of both beads
\[2x 20 µl each type\]). Added 480 µl of both anti-EpCAM and 480 µl
anti-CD45 beads, respectively, to the cell suspension and incubated 30
min at +4C shielded from light.

Washed the cells with 24 ml MACS buffer and centrifuged 1500 rpm (408 g)
for 5 min at +4C. Lastly, the cells were resuspended in 500µl x 4
(fractions) of MACS buffer.

Placed three LS columns and 30 µm filters in the magnetic MACS
Separator. Prepared them by washing them with 3 ml of MACS buffer, then
applied the cell suspension onto the column, and washed with 3 x 3 ml

nd 30 µm filters in the magnetic MACS
Separator. Prepared them by washing them with 3 ml of MACS buffer, then
applied the cell suspension onto the column, and washed with 3 x 3 ml
MACS buffer.

Columns were removed from the separator and placed on a new 15 ml falcon
tube. 5 ml MACS buffer was added to the column and with firmly pushing
the plunger into the column, magnetically labeled cells were flushed
out.

All cell suspensions were centrifuged 1500 rpm g for 5 min. Resuspended
the flow through cells in 5mL icPDC media. Resuspended the column bound
cells in 10 mL DMEM/F12+++ media. Cells in icPDC media
(fibroblast-enriched fraction) were then subjected to dead cell removal
(see next section), while the cells resuspended in DMEM/F12+++ (cancer
and immune cell-enriched fraction) were used for biobanking (see
biobanking section).

**[Dead Cell Removal]{.underline}**

Dead cell depletion: cells are magnetically labeled with Dead Cell
Removal MicroBeads and passed through a separation column. The unlabeled
living cells run through.

Overview:

1. Prepare 1x Binding Buffer by dilution of 20x Binding Buffer Stock
 > Solution with sterile, double-distiled water, 25 mL final volume
 > of 1x Binding Buffer was prepared, 1.25 mL of Binding Buffer +
 > 23.75mL of ddH2O.

2. Collect cells, centrifuge at 300 g and remove supernatant
 > completely.

3. Resuspend cells with Dead cell Removal Microbeads, 541.4 μl beads
 > accounting for 54.14E6 cells. Mix well and incubate for 15 min at
 > room temperature.

4. Place column in a MACs Separator. Prepare by rinsing with 1x Binding
 > Buffer.

5. Apply cell suspensions onto the column. Rinse with 1x Binding
 > Buffer.

6. Collect effluent as live cell fraction.

# Sample 604_pOme 

**09.03.2026**

Objective: **ADC titration experiment** using **Datopotamab deruxtecan
and its ADC control (human IgG1-deruxtecan, DAR4**, five concentrations
each) and **Sacituzumab tirumotecan and its ADC control (human
IgG1--sulfo-SPDB-DM4**, five concentrations each).

**Day 0**

**A) Dissociation**

The sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase,
followed by Collagenase & Hyaluronidase (C&H) Foteini.

The sample was divided in two parts and the dispase dissociation
procedure was applied to both parts as follows:

Dispase dissociation media included 19,4 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12
supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 400 µl
Dispase + 200 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C rotating for 30 min, vortexed
before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

**Day 3**

**Harvesting:**

**7,5** mL of Dispase II solution (7,35 mL DMEM/F12+++, 0,15 mL Dispase
II) was prepared. Media from the wells was aspirated. Cells were
resuspended with the Dispase II solution (one well per condition at the
same time as the corresponding well containing icPDC media). The omentum
gel droplets were broken up by scraping with a cell scraper and
pipetting up and down several times using a 1 mL pipette.The media from
the second corresponding well within the same condition was aspirated,
after which the cells from the first well were transferred to the second
well within the same condition, and the same procedure was applied. The
cells were incubated in a cell culture incubator for 30 min.The cell
suspensions were then transferred into 15 mL Falcon tubes containing 2.5
mL of DMEM/F12+++ medium. Additionally, the corresponding wells were
washed twice with 1 mL of DMEM/F12+++ medium, and the washes were
transferred to the corresponding 15 mL Falcon tubes. The cells were
centrifuged at 400 × g for 6 min at +4 °C.1 mL of pre-warmed TrypLE was
added to each tube and incubated for 5 min in a water bath, with the

ing 15 mL Falcon tubes. The cells were
centrifuged at 400 × g for 6 min at +4 °C.1 mL of pre-warmed TrypLE was
added to each tube and incubated for 5 min in a water bath, with the
suspension mixed twice during incubation. Then, 3 mL of DMEM/F12+++
medium was added to each Falcon tube, followed by centrifugation at 400
× g for 6 min at +4 °C.Finally, the cells were resuspended in FACS
buffer and transferred into 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes for cell counting and
flow cytometry.

**Note (1)**: We did not proceed with flow cytometry since these cells
were used for plating training and afterwards they were discarded.

**Νote (2):** The plates that were used were the Cellstar 6 well
suspension culture plates Cat-No: 662 102 by mistake, instead of the
CytoOne 6 well plates Cat-No: CC7672-7506.

**Day 3**

**14.03.2026**

**[Harvesting]{.underline}**

60 mL of Dispase II solution (58.2 mL DMEM/F12+++, 1.2 mL Dispase II,
and 600 µL DNase) was prepared. The medium was aspirated from the wells,
and the cells were resuspended in the Dispase II solution (one well per
condition at the same time as the corresponding well containing icPDC
medium). The omentum gel droplets were disrupted by scraping with a cell
scraper and pipetting up and down several times using a 1 mL pipette.
The medium from the second corresponding well within the same condition
was then aspirated, after which the cells from the first well were
transferred to the second well, and the same procedure was repeated. The
cells were incubated in a cell culture incubator for 30 minutes.The cell
suspensions were subsequently transferred into 15 mL Falcon tubes
containing 2.5 mL of DMEM/F12+++ medium. The corresponding wells were
washed twice with 1 mL of DMEM/F12+++ medium, and the washes were

quently transferred into 15 mL Falcon tubes
containing 2.5 mL of DMEM/F12+++ medium. The corresponding wells were
washed twice with 1 mL of DMEM/F12+++ medium, and the washes were
combined with the respective Falcon tubes. The cells were centrifuged at
400 × g for 6 minutes at +4 °C.Next, 1 mL of pre-warmed TrypLE was added
to each tube, followed by incubation for 5 minutes in a water bath, with
the suspension mixed twice during incubation. Then, 3 mL of DMEM/F12+++
medium was added to each tube, and the cells were centrifuged again at
400 × g for 6 minutes at +4 °C.Finally, the cells were resuspended in
FACS buffer and transferred into 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes for cell
counting and flow cytometry.

**[Flow cytometry:]{.underline}**

4°C for 6 min.

table:

-The multicolor sample pellets were resuspended in 50μL of these
antibody mixes

-The unstained control pellet was resuspended in 50μL of Brillian Stain
Buffer (#563794, BD Biosciences).

-All conditions were incubated for 30 min at 4°C, protected form light.

-Washed once by adding 900 μL FACs buffer, centrifuge 6 min 400g at +
4°C, and discarded supernatant.

-Added 200 µl of IC Fixation buffer (#00-8222 Invitrogen) and incubated
for 40 min at +4°C shielded from light.

-Washed once by adding 900 µL FACS buffer, centrifuge 6 min 400 g at
+4°C, and discard supernatant.

-Resuspend in 800 µl FACS buffer and stored overnight in the fridge

-The next day the tubes were centrifuged for 6 min 400 g at +4°C and the
pellet in 370 µl FACS buffer.

-Cell suspensions were pipetted through a 5 mL Flowcytometry polystyrene
tube with a cell-strainer cap (blue).

ry polystyrene

**[Single-Color Compensation Staining:]{.underline}**

-1 drop each of positive (BD CompBeads Anti-Mouse IgG, #51-90-9001229)
and negative (CompBeads Negative Control, #51-90-9001291) compensation
beads were added to 5 ml polystyrene round-bottom tubes (white cap).

-To each tube labeled for a single fluorochrome-conjugated antibody, 2
µl of the respective antibody was added (see flow cytometry panel above;
note: DCM-BV510 was replaced with CD16-BV510; 2.5 µl for EpCAM).

-Compensation tubes were incubated for 30 min at 4°C, protected from
light, followed by the addition of 350 µl FACS buffer.

**Note**: Compensation controls were prepared on Day 3 and used on Day
4. They were kept at + 4°C and protected from light and covered with
foil from the beginning. .

he sample was minced into small pieces and dissociated using Dispase
and Collagenase & Hyaluronidase by Foteini.

Dispase dissociation media included 19,4 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12
supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/strep) + 400 µl
Dispase + 200 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C rotating for 30 min, vortexed
before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm cell strainer.

Further dissociated the leftover tissue pieces with 17,8 ml DMEM/F12+++,
2 ml Collagenase & Hyaluronidase, and 200 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37°C,
rotating for 1 hour, vortexed before and after. Filtered through a 70 µm
cell strainer.

\(1\) First RBC lysis with 3 ml for 5 min + 15 ml DMEM/F12+++. (1)
Second RBC lysis with 2 ml for 5 min + 10 ml DMEM/F12+++. The final
pellet was resuspended in 3 mL of DMEM/F12+++ media.

**Sequentially dissociated cells:** (1) First RBC lysis with 2 ml for 5
min + 10 ml DMEM/F12+++. (1) Second RBC lysis with 2 ml for 5 min + 10
ml DMEM/F12+++. The final pellet was resuspended in 3 mL of icPDC media.
